metadata.title: Mapping Joyce — Ulysses
metadata.note: Original dataset compiled for mappingJoyce from the public-domain text of Ulysses (1922). Coordinates via OpenStreetMap/Nominatim.

properties.name: Martello Tower, Sandycove
properties.gloss: Stephen Dedalus, Buck Mulligan and Haines lodge here; the novel opens on its gun-platform.
properties.quote: Stately, plump Buck Mulligan came from the stairhead, bearing a bowl of lather on which a mirror and a razor lay crossed.

properties.name: The Forty Foot
properties.gloss: The men's bathing place below the tower where Mulligan goes to swim.

properties.name: Mr Deasy's school, Dalkey
properties.gloss: Stephen teaches a morning history class and is paid by the headmaster Mr Deasy.

properties.name: Sandymount Strand
properties.gloss: Stephen walks the tideline alone, meditating on perception and the 'ineluctable modality of the visible.'
properties.quote: Ineluctable modality of the visible: at least that if no more, thought through my eyes.

properties.name: 7 Eccles Street
properties.gloss: Leopold and Molly Bloom's home; the Odyssey of Bloom's day begins and ends here.
properties.quote: Mr Leopold Bloom ate with relish the inner organs of beasts and fowls.

properties.name: St George's Church
properties.gloss: Its bells (‘Heigho! Heigho!’) sound the hour over Bloom's breakfast.

properties.name: Sweny's Pharmacy
properties.gloss: Bloom buys the lemon soap that he carries through the day.

properties.name: Westland Row Station
properties.gloss: Formerly Westland Row; Bloom collects a letter under the name Henry Flower.

properties.name: Glasnevin Cemetery
properties.gloss: Paddy Dignam's funeral; Bloom rides out in the carriage and broods on death.

properties.name: Freeman's Journal office
properties.gloss: The newspaper offices where Bloom tries to place an ad; the episode is set in the rhetoric of headlines.

properties.name: Nelson's Pillar (site)
properties.gloss: The old Pillar on Sackville (O'Connell) Street; hub of the trams in the 'Aeolus' wind imagery.

properties.name: Davy Byrne's
properties.gloss: Bloom lunches on a gorgonzola sandwich and a glass of burgundy in the 'moral pub.'
properties.quote: He entered Davy Byrne's. Moral pub. He doesn't chat. Stands a drink now and then.

properties.name: National Library of Ireland
properties.gloss: Stephen expounds his Hamlet theory to the librarians; Bloom passes between the doors.

properties.name: Ormond Hotel
properties.gloss: The barmaids Miss Douce and Miss Kennedy; music and song fill the 'Sirens' episode.

properties.name: Barney Kiernan's (site)
properties.gloss: The pub of the nameless narrator and the hostile Citizen; Bloom is driven out under a hail of abuse.

properties.name: Star of the Sea Church, Sandymount
properties.gloss: Its evening service counterpoints Gerty MacDowell on the strand below.

properties.name: National Maternity Hospital, Holles Street
properties.gloss: Mina Purefoy gives birth; the prose gestates through the history of English styles.

properties.name: Nighttown (Mabbot Street)
properties.gloss: The brothel quarter (Monto); the hallucinatory 'Circe' drama. Mabbot Street is now Corporation Street/James Joyce Street.

properties.name: Cabman's shelter, Butt Bridge
properties.gloss: Bloom takes the exhausted Stephen for coffee in the small hours.

properties.name: 7 Eccles Street (return)
properties.gloss: Bloom, locked out, climbs over the area railings and lets Stephen in; the catechism of 'Ithaca.'
